verdict.answer_first: A states the answer in the first two words ('Plain Python.'), while B uses a full sentence to make the same point.
verdict.decisiveness: A makes a stronger, more decisive claim ('pandas is usually the wrong tool') than B's more moderate 'default to plain Python'.
verdict.fabrication: B invents file names ('in.csv', 'out.csv') and column names ('email', 'name') for its code example.
verdict.actionability: B is more actionable because its code snippet is a ready-to-adapt template for the exact task described, saving the time of writing boilerplate.
